title: Matelea rhamnifolia
taxonomy: Matelea rhamnifolia is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family consists of over 5,000 species of flowering plants. Matelea rhamnifolia is a species of flowering plant native to tropical regions. It belongs to the genus Matelea, which comprises approximately 220 species of vines and shrubs. The Apocynaceae family is known for its diverse range of plants, including ornamental and medicinal species.
uses: Matelea rhamnifolia is reported to have potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant is believed to be used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems.
